message:
feat(fcp-core): add Post-Compromise Security (PCS) zones via TreeKEM ratcheting

Introduce an MLS/TreeKEM-inspired epoch ratcheting module for sensitive
zones (z:owner, z:private) that provides forward security recovery after
compromise detection. When a compromised member is removed and the group
rekeys, all subsequent epoch keys are cryptographically independent of
any material the attacker could have captured.

Key design decisions:
- Uses the core TreeKEM binary-tree key derivation primitives directly
  rather than full RFC 9420 MLS, since personal meshes (n=3..10 nodes)
  don't justify the overhead
- Zone key material derived via HKDF-SHA256 from group secret + epoch
- ZoneKeyManifest carries PcsMode flag and group state references
- Maximum group size capped at 32 (PCS_MAX_GROUP_SIZE)

The module includes comprehensive error handling (PcsError) covering
group size limits, member lookup, epoch mismatches, empty groups, tree
index bounds, and removed-member access prevention.

- pcs.rs: full TreeKEM ratcheting implementation (~43KB)
- lib.rs: expose as pub mod pcs
- Cargo.toml: add criterion dev-dependency + bench target
- pcs_benchmarks.rs: criterion benchmarks for PCS operations
- Cargo.lock: updated deps (clap 4.6.0, darling 0.23.0,
  serde_with 3.18.0, tracing-subscriber 0.3.23, etc.)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
